title: All Six March 31 Finals Confirmed: Italy vs Bosnia, Sweden vs Poland, Turkey vs Kosovo, Denmark vs Czechia, DR Congo vs Jamaica, Iraq vs Bolivia
detail: Following the decisive March 26 semifinals, all four UEFA World Cup playoff finals and both FIFA intercontinental finals for March 31 are now confirmed. Italy face Bosnia-Herzegovina in Zenica (Path A); Sweden meet Poland in Solna (Path B); Kosovo host Turkey in Pristina (Path C); Czechia take on Denmark in Prague (Path D). In the FIFA intercontinental playoffs, DR Congo face Jamaica and Iraq play Bolivia — all six matches determining the final six World Cup berths that complete the 48-team field for the tournament beginning June 11 in Mexico City at Estadio Azteca.

title: Viktor Gyökeres Hat-trick vs Ukraine Marks Sweden's Star Turn on Path to World Cup
detail: Viktor Gyökeres's stunning hat-trick against Ukraine in Sweden's 3–0 victory dominated post-match coverage on March 27, with pundits declaring the Arsenal striker one of the most dangerous forwards heading into the tournament if Sweden qualify on March 31. Gyökeres — who scored 43 goals for Arsenal in the 2024–25 season — had been considered a doubt amid fitness speculation but delivered a commanding display in Valencia. Sweden face Poland in the Path B final on March 31 at Friends Arena in Solna. Kosovo's 4–3 victory over Slovakia was also widely described as the upset of the playoff round.
